expected_behavior: Launches 6 parallel specialized review agents
Covers code quality, type safety, security, tests, backend, and frontend
Produces structured review report with status per area
Uses conventional comment prefixes (praise, nitpick, suggestion, issue)

evaluations.query: Review PR #99 with focus on security vulnerabilities
expected_behavior: Prioritizes the security-auditor agent for vulnerability-focused review
Checks for secrets and credentials in diff
Scans for injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS)
Validates authentication and authorization checks are properly enforced

evaluations.query: Give me a quick review of PR #15
expected_behavior: Uses single code-quality-reviewer agent only for faster turnaround
Skips deep multi-agent analysis to reduce review time
Provides high-level review summary with key findings highlighted
Completes significantly faster than a full multi-agent review

evaluations.query: Review PR #77 that adds LLM integration code
expected_behavior: Adds 7th agent: llm-integrator for AI code review
Checks prompt injection prevention measures in LLM integration code
Validates token limit handling and truncation strategies are implemented
Reviews caching strategy and error fallbacks
Checks TypeScript strict mode compliance across all changed files
Validates Zod or Pydantic schema usage
Flags any types or type assertions
Verifies exhaustive switch/union handling with never-type assertions

evaluations.query: I need to review a PR that touches both backend architecture and frontend components. Should I use agent teams?
expected_behavior: Evaluates whether the PR review requires cross-cutting communication between review agents
Recommends Agent Teams when reviewers need mesh communication across backend and frontend
Uses TeamCreate with peer-to-peer SendMessage for coordinated multi-agent review efforts
Warns about higher cost of Agent Teams approximately 2.5x compared to sequential Task tool review

evaluations.query: Review PR #200 that only changes a single constant value from 30 to 60
expected_behavior: Does not launch all 6 parallel agents for a trivial one-line change to avoid unnecessary cost
Still checks if the constant change has downstream impacts like timeout behavior or rate limit thresholds
Uses the quick single-agent review path rather than the full multi-agent orchestration

evaluations.query: Review PR #99999 which does not exist in this repository
expected_behavior: Detects that the PR number does not exist and reports a clear error instead of failing silently
Does not spawn review agents before confirming the PR is accessible and has a valid diff
Suggests verifying the PR number and repository, or listing open PRs to find the correct one

evaluations.query: Review PR #55 that changes GitHub Actions workflow permissions from read to write and adds a new secret
expected_behavior: Flags workflow permission escalation as a security-sensitive change requiring extra scrutiny
Checks that the new secret is not hardcoded in the workflow file and uses GitHub encrypted secrets
Uses security [blocking] label for any workflow change that broadens repository or token permissions
